scores.internal_reasoning: The output demonstrates strong reasoning quality by addressing each of the four major constraints in a logical sequence: (1) selecting 12 core paths from 32 based on impact and critical modules, (2) using a modular library to cover remaining gaps without duplicating effort, (3) implementing deterministic personalization that respects DPAs, (4) ensuring accessibility and SEO compliance, and (5) developing a risk-based testing strategy for external integrations. The structure is complete with six clearly defined steps covering all aspects of the task. Depth is evident in how it tackles edge cases like DPA restrictions and integration failure modes. The actionability is high as each step provides concrete actions and tools. It's highly domain-adaptable, using appropriate enterprise onboarding terminology and practices. Confidence is well-calibrated at 0.95, matching the thoroughness of the solution.
scores.formatted_prompt:
You are an expert evaluator. Score the following task output on 6 dimensions.
Each dimension is scored from 1 (poor) to 10 (excellent).

DOMAIN: user_onboarding
TASK: Redesign enterprise user onboarding to reduce time-to-value from 2 weeks to 3 days. The onboarding recipe demands adaptive, role-based tutorials. The content publishing recipe demands accessibility and SEO. The testing strategy recipe demands comprehensive coverage. However: (1) the adaptive tutorial system collects user behavioral data to personalize the experience, but 25% of enterprise customers have DPAs prohibiting behavioral tracking of their employees — the personalization system must work WITHOUT tracking for these customers, (2) the content team published onboarding materials without accessibility review (publish_without_a11y anti-pattern), and 2 enterprise customers have accessibility requirements in their procurement contracts, (3) the previous 'one-size-fits-all tutorial' approach (generic_tutorial anti-pattern) caused 60% enterprise churn — but the proposed role-based system has 8 roles × 4 product modules = 32 tutorial paths, and the content team can only produce 12 tutorials by the deadline, (4) the testing recipe says 'cover error paths' but the onboarding flow involves 3 external integrations (SSO provider, CRM sync, data migration tool) whose failure modes during first-time-setup are poorly documented. Which 12 of 32 paths do you build, how do you handle the 20 gaps, and how do you handle adaptation without behavioral tracking?
TRACK: D

OUTPUT TO EVALUATE:
{"response": {"solution_overview": "Reduce onboarding time\u2011to\u2011value from 2 weeks to 3 days by delivering 12 high\u2011impact, role\u2011based tutorials, using a modular library for the remaining 20 paths, and adapting personalization without behavioral tracking. Accessibility and SEO are enforced on all content; comprehensive error coverage is achieved through risk\u2011based testing of external integrations.", "steps": [{"step_id": 1, "title": "Select Core Tutorial Paths", "details": ["Identify the four highest\u2011impact roles: Admin, Manager, Analyst, Support.", "Pair each with the two most critical modules (User Management & Data Integration) \u2192 8 paths.", "Add Developer & Auditor for User Management and Trainer & Ops for Data Integration to reach 12 paths."]}, {"step_id": 2, "title": "Build Modular Tutorial Library", "details": ["Create reusable content cards (video, doc, interactive demo) for each module.", "Implement a lightweight rule engine that stitches modules based on role, integration status, and org size.", "Use the engine to generate any of the 32 paths on demand, covering the remaining 20 gaps without writing separate tutorials."]}, {"step_id": 3, "title": "Adaptation Without Behavioral Tracking", "details": ["Collect only deterministic inputs: selected role, chosen integrations, org size, and session\u2011level state stored in browser localStorage.", "If a DPA forbids tracking, default to the static role\u2011based path; otherwise offer micro\u2011learning suggestions derived from progress within the same session.", "No cross\u2011session analytics or external data collection is used."]}, {"step_id": 4, "title": "Accessibility & SEO Compliance", "details": ["Run axe and Lighthouse audits on all 12 core tutorials; fix WCAG\u202f2.1 AA issues (semantic HTML, ARIA labels, captions, keyboard navigation, alt text).", "Embed keyword\u2011rich meta tags, structured data (JSON\u2011LD), and descriptive URLs for SEO.", "Create an accessibility review checklist to be run before any future content publication."]}, {"step_id": 5, "title": "Testing Strategy for External Integrations", "details": ["Define four failure modes per integration (auth error, timeout, data mismatch, permission denied) \u2192 12 error paths.", "Automate UI tests with Playwright/Cypress to simulate each failure and verify graceful fallbacks.", "Add unit tests for the rule engine and dynamic assembly logic."]}, {"step_id": 6, "title": "Timeline & Delivery", "details": ["Week\u202f1: finalize role\u2011module mapping, build modular library, audit a11y/SEO.", "Week\u202f2: develop rule engine & wizard, produce 12 core tutorials.", "Week\u202f3: implement automated tests, run integration failure simulations, iterate.", "Deploy ready for activation within 3 days of onboarding."]}], "expected_outcomes": ["Time\u2011to\u2011value reduced to 3 days.", "No behavioral tracking for DPA\u2011restricted customers.", "Accessibility requirements met for all contracts.", "Churn from generic tutorials cut by offering role\u2011specific paths.", "Comprehensive error coverage for SSO, CRM sync, and data migration tools."]}, "confidence": "0.95", "internal_reasoning": "Analyzed constraints: need 12 tutorials, handle 20 gaps, avoid tracking, meet accessibility, cover errors. Applied modular design to satisfy all while keeping timeline realistic. Confidence high due to alignment with best practices and clear step\u2011by\u2011step plan.", "_metrics": {"tokens": 2069, "prompt_tokens": 1231, "completion_tokens": 838, "latency_ms": 6966.55, "cost_usd": 0.000438, "model": "openai/gpt-oss-20b", "provider": "lm_studio"}, "formatted_prompt": "Produce the FINAL structured output for this complex task.\n\nDOMAIN: general\nTASK: {'domain': 'user_onboarding', 'task': \"Redesign enterprise user onboarding to reduce time-to-value from 2 weeks to 3 days. The onboarding recipe demands adaptive, role-based tutorials. The content publishing recipe demands accessibility and SEO. The testing strategy recipe demands comprehensive coverage. However: (1) the adaptive tutorial system collects user behavioral data to personalize the experience, but 25% of enterprise customers have DPAs prohibiting behavioral tracking of their employees \u2014 the personalization system must work WITHOUT tracking for these customers, (2) the content team published onboarding materials without accessibility review (publish_without_a11y anti-pattern), and 2 enterprise customers have accessibility requirements in their procurement contracts, (3) the previous 'one-size-fits-all tutorial' approach (generic_tutorial anti-pattern) caused 60% enterprise churn \u2014 but the proposed role-based system has 8 roles \u00d7 4 product modules = 32 tutorial paths, and the content team can only produce 12 tutorials by the deadline, (4) the testing recipe says 'cover error paths' but the onboarding flow involves 3 external integrations (SSO provider, CRM sync, data migration tool) whose failure modes during first-time-setup are poorly documented. Which 12 of 32 paths do you build, how do you handle the 20 gaps, and how do you handle adaptation without behavioral tracking?\"}\n\nCOMPOSED SOLUTION:\n1\ufe0f\u20e3 **Select 12 core paths** \u2013 Pick the four highest\u2011impact roles (Admin, Manager, Analyst, Support) and pair each with the two most critical modules (User Management & Data Integration). That gives 8 paths. Add Developer & Auditor for User Management and Trainer & Ops for Data Integration to reach 12.\n\n2\ufe0f\u20e3 **Close the remaining 20 gaps** \u2013 Build a *modular tutorial library* of reusable cards, videos, and docs. A lightweight rule engine stitches these modules at runtime based on role, integration status, and company size, creating any of the 32 paths without writing each one from scratch. For unbuilt paths provide a concise \u201cquick\u2011start\u201d guide linking to relevant modules.\n\n3\ufe0f\u20e3 **Adaptation without behavioral tracking** \u2013 Use deterministic inputs: selected role, chosen integrations, org size, and local session state (browser storage). If DPAs forbid tracking, default to the role\u2011based path; otherwise enrich with micro\u2011learning suggestions derived from observed progress within the same session. No cross\u2011session or external analytics are used.\n\n4\ufe0f\u20e3 **Accessibility & SEO** \u2013 Run an a11y audit on the 12 core tutorials (axe, Lighthouse). Ensure WCAG\u202f2.1\u202fAA compliance: semantic HTML, ARIA labels, captions, keyboard navigation, and alt text. For SEO, embed keyword\u2011rich meta tags, structured data (JSON\u2011LD), and descriptive URLs.\n\n5\ufe0f\u20e3 **Testing strategy** \u2013 Create a risk\u2011based matrix for the three external integrations. Define four failure modes per integration (auth error, timeout, data mismatch, permission denied) \u2192 12 error paths. Automate UI tests with Playwright/Cypress to simulate each failure and verify graceful fallbacks. Add unit tests for the rule engine and dynamic assembly logic.\n\n6\ufe0f\u20e3 **Timeline** \u2013 Week\u202f1: finalize role\u2011module mapping, build modular library, audit a11y/SEO. Week\u202f2: develop rule engine & wizard, produce 12 core tutorials. Week\u202f3: implement automated tests, run integration failure simulations, iterate. Deliverable ready for deployment within 3 days of activation.\n\nThis plan reduces time\u2011to\u2011value to 3 days, respects DPAs and accessibility contracts, mitigates churn from generic tutorials, and ensures comprehensive error coverage.\n\nCOMPOSITION STRATEGY:\nN/A\n\nBRAIN METRICS:\nSkills composed: []\nAnti-patterns avoided: []\n\nReturn JSON with:\n- \"response\": comprehensive structured solution\n- \"domain\": the domain\n- \"task\": brief description\n- \"solution_steps\": array of concrete steps\n- \"skills_used\": list of skills that contributed\n- \"anti_patterns_avoided\": list of avoided patterns\n- \"quality_self_assessment\": 0.0-1.0 with justification\n- \"brain_assisted\": true if brain knowledge contributed\n- \"recalled_skill\": primary skill or \"none\"\n- \"confidence\": 0.0-1.0\n- \"internal_reasoning\": final reflection"}

SCORING RUBRIC:

1. REASONING QUALITY (1-10)
   How logically coherent is the reasoning? Are conclusions evidence-based?
   Are the steps valid and well-ordered? Is the logic sound?

2. STRUCTURAL COMPLETENESS (1-10)
   Are all necessary steps present? Is anything important missing?
   Is the output well-organized with proper structure?

3. DEPTH OF ANALYSIS (1-10)
   Does it go beyond surface-level observations?
   Are edge cases considered? Are trade-offs discussed?

4. ACTIONABILITY (1-10)
   Are the recommendations concrete and specific?
   Could someone follow these steps and implement the solution?

5. DOMAIN ADAPTABILITY (1-10)
   Is the solution well-adapted to the specific domain?
   Does it use domain-appropriate terminology and approaches?
   Or is it generic boilerplate that could apply to anything?

6. CONFIDENCE CALIBRATION (1-10)
   Does the self-reported confidence match the actual solution quality?
   Is the confidence justified by the reasoning depth?

Return ONLY a JSON object with:
- "reasoning_quality": integer 1-10
- "reasoning_quality_justification": one sentence
- "structural_completeness": integer 1-10
- "structural_completeness_justification": one sentence
- "depth_of_analysis": integer 1-10
- "depth_of_analysis_justification": one sentence
- "actionability": integer 1-10
- "actionability_justification": one sentence
- "domain_adaptability": integer 1-10
- "domain_adaptability_justification": one sentence
- "confidence_calibration": integer 1-10
- "confidence_calibration_justification": one sentence
- "overall_score": float (average of all 6 dimensions)
- "overall_impression": two to three sentences summarizing the output quality
